reasoning: The market price on October 16 was 57%, putting the baseline probability around a coin flip. Since then, the price of Bitcoin reached a local low of $103,600 on October 17. A barrier of $100,000 is only about a 3.5%-8% move depending on exactly where prices are in late October (likely ~105k-110k). Given Bitcoin's historical volatility (even at a lower end of 30-40% annualized) and the specified resolution source (Binance 1-minute candles, which are notoriously prone to low and sudden liquidating "wicks" during flash crashes), a touch of 99,999.99 is highly probable within the remaining 66 days of 2025. Although strong bullish sentiment and a 25 bps rate cut expectation might give upward momentum, the volatility and frequent regular drawdowns (like the 8-12% seen earlier in the year) keep the one-touch probability robust. I forecast 61%, resting slightly above the October 16 Polymarket baseline to account for the recent dip toward 103k and the inclusion of high intra-day volatility in the 1-minute candle criteria.
